title: Maintain fraud-scoring system reliability through Q4 holiday traffic
description: Own end-to-end reliability of the fraud-scoring system through the Q4 traffic peak (Black Friday → Boxing Day → Lunar New Year). 18-month-old system with no major incidents in that window. Includes capacity planning, on-call coverage, runbook updates, and the Stripe API migration (FRAUD-89) which lands mid-window without downtime.

comments.author: jonas
comments.body: This is a continuation of what Rayan has been doing solo for ~18 months. Tagging FRAUD-44 to make the work visible for the next promo cycle — none of this shows up in commit-count metrics.

comments.author: rayan
comments.body: Q4 wrap: 0 P1s, 1 P2 (resolved in 22 min), 0 customer-visible outages. Stripe migration cut over on 11/04 with zero downtime. $2M+/day stable.

title: Migrate legacy payment processor to Stripe Payment Intents
description: Replace the legacy in-house payment processor with Stripe's Payment Intents API. Maintain compatibility with existing fraud-rules engine. Zero-downtime cutover required.

comments.author: rayan
comments.body: Wrote up the migration plan in docs/rfcs/0011-stripe-migration.md. Two-phase: dual-write window, then shadow-compare, then cut. Estimated 8 weeks; landed in 9.

comments.author: priya
comments.body: Want to make sure the fraud-rules eval still hits <50ms p99 after the migration. Let's add a perf gate to the cutover criteria.

comments.author: rayan
comments.body: Added. Cutover criteria now includes p99 fraud-rule eval ≤ 50ms over a rolling 1-hour window before we flip the flag.

title: Partition transactions table by month
description: Follow-up from PR #1340. Composite index on (merchant_id, created_at) is growing fast at current volume. Plan: monthly partitioning with a rolling 13-month window; older partitions archived to cold storage. Keeps hot read path fast while bounding index size.

title: Surface Fraud Platform services in Developer Portal
description: Cross-team contribution. Platform Eng was bandwidth-limited; Rayan picked this up to unblock the Q1 portal launch. Adds fraud-scoring-api, rules-engine, payment-gateway, ledger-sync to the internal catalog with on-call info and dependency map.

comments.author: sarah-chen
comments.body: Thanks for picking this up! Saved my team about a week.
